protocol.name: layer_edge_resize
protocol.copyright:

interface.name: layer_edge_resize_manager_v1

description.summary: opt-in compositor-drawn edge resize for layer surfaces

interface.description: Lets a layer-shell client opt a surface into a compositor-drawn, VSCode-style edge resize sash on its outer (non-anchored) edge. The compositor infers the resizable edge from the surface's anchor, draws a themed indicator on hover near that edge, shows an EW-resize cursor, and on press+drag shows a ghost that the surface springs to on release. The resize is fully compositor-driven; the client only opts in and supplies width bounds.

request.name: destroy
request.description: Destroying the manager does not affect already-created layer_edge_resize_v1 objects.

entry.name: already_exists
entry.summary: an edge_resize object already exists for this surface

request.name: get_edge_resize

description.summary: enable edge resize for a layer surface

request.description: Create an edge-resize object for the given layer surface. Only one such object may exist per surface; a second request raises already_exists. The surface is expected to be a layer-shell surface anchored to a single vertical edge (left or right).

arg.name: surface
arg.summary: the layer surface to make edge-resizable

interface.name: layer_edge_resize_v1
interface.description: Carries the width bounds for one edge-resizable layer surface. Bounds are in logical pixels and take effect immediately (no commit required); they constrain the next drag. Defaults are compositor-defined until set.

description.summary: minimum surface width in logical px
maximum surface width in logical px (0 = output width)

arg.name: width
arg.summary: maximum width, logical px; 0 means full output width

description.summary: disable edge resize for this surface
